title: Fix image upload error handling with quality constraints
description: When users upload low-resolution or highly compressed images in the chat interface, they receive a generic 'Upload failed' error instead of an actionable message. The error handling defaults to retry 3 times on status=0 before showing generic error. Need to add client-side image dimension validation with actionable error messages and improve error extraction from Vercel Blob responses.
questions: What should be the minimum image dimension requirement (width/height in pixels)?
Should we reject images below the threshold or attempt processing with a warning?
Is there a specific file size limit beyond which we should reject low-resolution images?
